title: centaurea rupestris subsp. finazzeri
intro: Centaurea rupestris subsp. finazzeri, also known as Finazzi's mountain cornflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of Italy and has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The plant is used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, due to its high content of flavonoids and terpenoids. Herbal preparations of Centaurea rupestris subsp. finazzeri can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
